hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 residues (e.g., 192/193/205/284), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/5) electrostatic interactions while being tolerated in SSTR2. Position 11 (currently Q) will be mutated to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) to better fit the SSTR2-specific TM6 pocket (residue 272/273), improving selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong SSTR2 binding but fail to sufficiently disrupt off-target interactions. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are necessary to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5). Focusing design on these positions allows disruption of conserved pocket contacts without compromising SSTR2 binding.
Introducing charged or hydrophobic residues at these positions can disrupt conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving pharmacophore integrity.
